name: Rugelach/hershey's
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: www.hersheys.com
1. In a large bowl, beat softened butter and cream cheese on medium speed of an electric mixer until blended and smooth.
2. Gradually add flour, beating on low speed until well blended. Divide dough into 3 equal parts; wrap individually in plastic wrap. Refrigerate 3 to 4 hours or until firm enough to roll.
3. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
4. On a lightly floured surface, roll one piece of dough into a 9-inch circle (keep remaining dough refrigerated). Keep the rolled dough cold while working with it.
5. Cut the circle into 12 wedges. Place about 1 teaspoon of prepared filling at the wide end of each wedge; spread about three-fourths of the way up the wedge.
6. Starting at the wide end, roll toward the point. Place cookies, point sides down, about 1 inch apart on an ungreased cookie sheet.
7. Brush cookies with melted butter.
8. Stir together sugar and cinnamon; sprinkle over cookies. Bake for 15 to 20 minutes or until golden. Remove from the cookie sheet to a wire rack. Cool completely.
9. Repeat the procedure with remaining dough and filling. Approximately 3 dozen cookies are produced.
Cocoa Nut Filling:
In a small bowl, stir together 1-1/2 cups ground walnuts, 6 tablespoons sugar, and 3 tablespoons Hershey's Cocoa. Stir in 3 tablespoons milk. About 1 cup filling.
